title: Serum oxidant and antioxidant status during early and late recovery periods following an all-out 21-km run in trained adolescent runners.

title: Renal function parameters during early and late recovery periods following an all-out 21-km run in trained adolescent runners.
notes: Direct adolescent-runner biochemical time course; all-out 21 km; 2 h 4 h 24 h; no clearance inference.

title: Effect of Long-Duration Adventure Races on Cardiac Damage Biomarker Release and Muscular Function in Young Athletes.
notes: Youth indirect; 1-day and 2-day mixed-modality races; marker-specific 24-72 h time course.

title: Isometric Posterior Chain Peak Force Recovery Response Following Match-Play in Elite Youth Soccer Players: Associations with Relative Posterior Chain Strength.
notes: Youth athlete indirect; force at post 24 h 48 h 72 h; no subsequent training; large individual variability.

title: Static stretching does not enhance recovery in elite youth soccer players.
notes: Youth athlete indirect; static-stretch versus passive recovery; pre post 48 h; intervention confounds natural kinetics.

title: Recovery Patterns of Low-frequency Fatigue in Elite Youth Soccer Players after Official Matches.
notes: Youth athlete indirect; objective and subjective outcomes diverge at 24-48 h.

title: Post-Match Recovery Responses in Italian Serie A Youth Soccer Players: Effects of Manipulating Training Load 48 h After Match Play.
population: Italian Serie A youth soccer players
notes: Youth athlete indirect; complete versus reduced training at 48 h; 72-h state includes intervening load.

title: Impact of post-match fatigue on peak force in elite youth soccer players: Analysis of 48 to 72 hours post-match using the isometric mid-thigh pull exercise.
population: U19 and U23 elite soccer players
notes: Youth/young-adult indirect; IMTP force at 48 h and 72 h; intervening MD+2 load prevents match-only inference.

title: Acute effects of strength training interventions on subjective, neuromuscular, and biochemical fatigue parameters in elite youth soccer players.
population: Elite U17 and U19 soccer players
notes: Youth athlete indirect; fixed-order crossover; selected markers at 24 h 48 h 72 h; no safety inference.

title: Youths Are Less Susceptible to Exercise-Induced Muscle Damage Than Adults: A Systematic Review With Meta-Analysis.
notes: Youth indirect review; peak strength soreness and CK effects beyond 24 h; not a session-specific interval study.

title: National-Standard Middle-Distance Runners Maintain 1500 m Time Trial Running Performance on Successive Days.
notes: Runner direct but adult; two treadmill 1500-m trials 24 h apart; performance-specific counterevidence only.

title: Gait and Neuromuscular Changes Are Evident in Some Masters Club Level Runners 24-h After Interval Training Run.
notes: Runner direct but adult masters; six 800-m intervals; gait force and neuromuscular outcomes at post and 24 h.

title: Kinetics of recovery and normalization of running biomechanics following aerobic-based exercise-induced muscle damage in recreational male runners.
notes: Runner direct but adult; 60-min downhill run; gait CK pain and MRI at post 24 h 48 h.

title: Neuromuscular, biomechanical, and energetic adjustments following repeated bouts of downhill running.
notes: Runner direct but adult; two downhill bouts; post 24 h 48 h 72 h 96 h 168 h; explicit soreness beyond 72 h.

title: Recovery kinetics following sprint training: resisted versus unresisted sprints.
population: Competitive sprinters long jumpers and soccer players
notes: Sprint-trained adult indirect; unresisted versus 10% and 20% body-mass resisted sprints; 24 h 48 h 72 h.

title: Time course of changes in performance and inflammatory responses after acute plyometric exercise.
notes: Indirect; intense plyometric bout; post 24 h 48 h 72 h 96 h 120 h; participant details limited in abstract.

title: The acute effect of a multi-modal plyometric training session on field-specific performance measures.
notes: Indirect; n=9; combined session; performance soreness and CK at 24 h and 48 h.

title: Neuromuscular and autonomic function is fully recovered within 24 h following a sprint interval training session.
notes: Indirect cycling; title wording retained only as bibliographic identity; selected 24 h and 48 h markers cannot establish complete recovery.

title: Time Course of Recovery after Cycling Repeated Sprints.
notes: Indirect cycling; repeated sessions separated by 24 h or 48 h; performance and neuromuscular markers disagree at 24 h.

title: Acute effects of exercise-induced muscle damage on sprint and change of direction performance: A systematic review and meta-analysis.
notes: Indirect review; 20 studies; sprint COD CMJ CK and DOMS through 72 h; high heterogeneity.

title: Monitoring the Athlete Match Response: Can External Load Variables Predict Post-match Acute and Residual Fatigue in Soccer? A Systematic Review with Meta-analysis.
notes: Indirect review; 11 studies n=165; external load associations mainly at acute and 24 h not 48-72 h.

title: Postmatch recovery of physical performance and biochemical markers in team ball sports: a systematic review.
notes: Indirect review; 28 studies; performance and biochemical time courses to 144 h; full text available but not inspected in this extraction.
